answer:
Let the lengths of the parallel sides be \(2x\) cm and \(5x\) cm.  
The area of a trapezium is \(\frac{1}{2} \times (\text{sum of parallel sides}) \times \text{height}\), so  
\[
\frac{1}{2} \times (2x + 5x) \times 8 = 84.
\]  
Simplifying: \(4 \times 7x = 84\) gives \(28x = 84\), so \(x = 3\).  
Thus the bases are \(2 \times 3 = 6\) cm and \(5 \times 3 = 15\) cm.

\boxed{6 \text{ cm and } 15 \text{ cm}}
